data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: YouTube uses a microservices architecture to handle various functionalities like user management, video uploads, and recommendations.
data.technical_spec: Service-oriented architecture with independent services communicating over RESTful APIs.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: To efficiently deliver video content globally, YouTube employs a CDN that caches content closer to users.
data.technical_spec: Global cache servers, edge computing for latency reduction.

data.title: Data Infrastructure
data.description: Scalable data storage solutions for handling large volumes of video data and metadata.
data.technical_spec: Petabyte-scale distributed storage, databases like Bigtable.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Load balancers distribute incoming requests to different server clusters to maintain high availability.
data.technical_spec: Global load balancing with automatic failover.

data.title: Video Processing
data.description: Processes like transcoding are crucial for rendering videos in various resolutions and formats.
